SEREBRYANAYA RELIED ON AND/OR A DUTY OWED BY RIMKUS TO MS. SEREBRYANAYA. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR # 5976 =302/HK

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-30-2017 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Gene V. Halavanau, of Halavanau Law Office, Tel. 415.692.5301, for plaintiffs;
Maria M. Lampasona, of Lombardi, Loper & Conant, LLP, Tel. 510.433.2600, for defendants.

Ruling: A R G U E D. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT RIMKUS CONSULTING GROUP, INC.'s MOTION TO STRIKE portions of 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is taken off calendar in light of the court's ruling on Rimkus's demurrer that disposes of the sole cause of action asserted against Rimkus in the first amended complaint. Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Felicia Green; Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR No. 8189, email: ckaren@sftc.org; Dept. 302.

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-30-2017 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Gene V. Halavanau, of Halavanau Law Office, Tel. 415.692.5301, for plaintiffs;
Maria M. Lampasona, of Lombardi, Loper & Conant, LLP, Tel. 510.433.2600, for defendants.

Ruling: A R G U E D. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT RIMKUS CONSULTING GROUP, INC.'s DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT as to the third cause of action in the first amended complaint is sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiffs Marina Serebryanaya and Oleg Nemtsov's cause of action for concealment against Rimkus fails to allege that Rimkus had a duty to disclose the allegedly concealed facts which is required for a concealment claim. (Boschma v. Home Loan Center, Inc. (2011) 198 Cal.App.4th 230, 248.) While a duty to disclose can arise where the defendant alone has knowledge of material facts and the plaintiff does not have access to those material facts, the allegations in the first amended complaint preclude such a duty. Paragraphs 14 and 19 allege that defendant Homesite Insurance Company also knew of the same allegedly concealed facts. Because it does not appear reasonably possibly that plaintiffs can allege an actionable fraud claim against Rimkus, the demurrer is sustained without leave to amend. Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Felicia Green; Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR No. 8189, email: ckaren@sftc.org; Dept. 302.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-14-2017 Continued To Apr-05-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-03-2017 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. CHRISTOPHER CARLING OF THE KERLEY SCHAFFER LLP, TEL. NO. 1-510-379-5801 EXT. 202 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. MARIA M. LAMPASONA OF THE LOMBARDI LOPER & CONANT, LLP, TEL. NO. 1-510-433-2600 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; DEFENDANT RIMKUS CONSULTING GROUP, INC's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT -
Defendant Rimkus Consulting Group, Inc.'s demurrer to the third cause of action for aiding and abetting in the complaint is sustained with twenty days leave to amend. The third cause of action alleges that Rimkus aided Homesite in its failure to perform its obligations to plaintiff Marina Serebryanaya. That is not a cognizable claim for aiding and abetting because nothing in the complaint shows that Rimkus owed any duty to Ms. Serebryanaya. Ms. Serebryanaya's reliance on Bock v. Hansen (2014) 225 Cal. App. 4th 215 is inapposite since that case involved a misrepresentation of fact made to and relied on by the insured. Ms. Serebryanaya is given twenty days leave to file a cognizable claim against Rimkus, if she can do so in good faith, based on conduct committed by Rimkus that Ms. Serebryanaya relied on and/or a duty owed by Rimkus to Ms. Serebryanaya. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/hk
